channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: JUST IN: FG releases 2026 common entrance results
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has released the 2026 Common Entrance Examination results for unity schools. Find out top scorers and admission details here.
Read More: https://punchng.com/just-in-fg-releases-2026-common-entrance-results/

item.title: Your voices formed part of constitutional changes, Reps tell Youths
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives acknowledges that youths’ voices have influenced constitutional changes and urges active participation in the 2027 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/your-voices-formed-part-of-constitutional-changes-reps-tell-youths/

item.title: PHOTOS: Tinubu inaugurates Abuja city walk, pledges to protect foreign investments
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu inaugurates the Abuja City Walk, reaffirming his administration’s commitment to protecting local and foreign investments and making Nigeri
Read More: https://punchng.com/photos-tinubu-inaugurates-abuja-city-walk-pledges-to-protect-foreign-investments/

item.title: Cross River clears five-month salary arrears after Obudu Ranch workers’ protest
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River State Government clears five months of Obudu Ranch salaries and allowances after workers’ protest, boosting morale at the resort.
Read More: https://punchng.com/cross-river-clears-five-month-salary-arrears-after-obudu-ranch-workers-protest/

item.title: Mary Habila: APM asks Tinubu to suspend Umahi, order independent probe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Allied People’s Movement (APM) urges President Tinubu to suspend Works Minister David Umahi and order an independent probe into Mary Habila’s death.
Read More: https://punchng.com/mary-habila-apm-asks-tinubu-to-suspend-umahi-order-independent-probe/

item.title: UNIZIK denies lecturer’s involvement in viral sex scandal video
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nnamdi Azikiwe University (UNIZIK) denies claims linking its lecturer to a viral sex scandal video, stating the incident did not occur on campus.
Read More: https://punchng.com/unizik-denies-lecturers-involvement-in-viral-sex-scandal-video/

item.title: Oyebamiji flags off campaign, promises equitable resource distribution
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APC candidate Bola Oyebamiji flags off his Osun election campaign, promising equitable resource distribution, improved infrastructure, and civil service re
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyebamiji-flags-off-campaign-promises-equitable-resource-distribution/

item.title: Ogun Extends Work Palliative, Transport Allowance For Civil Servants
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Ogun State Governor, Prince Dapo Abiodun has approved the extension of the one-day-off-duty work palliative for eligible public and civil servants in the state. The Governor has also approved the payment of a ₦10,000 monthly transport allowance for an additional three months, covering July to Septem

item.title: Mary Habila: What she told her boyfriend before she died – Umahi breaks silence
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Minister of Works, David Umahi, on Thursday broke his silence on the circumstances surrounding the unfortunate death of Miss Mary Habila, a staff member who died at the minister’s residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State. The Minister at a press conference in Abuja revealed that Mary Habila spoke with h

item.title: Ex-Rivers gov Rotimi Amaechi’s mother dies at 89
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Rivers State Governor Rotimi Amaechi’s mother, Mary Amaechi, has died peacefully at 89. Learn more about the cherished matriarch’s life and legacy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ex-rivers-gov-rotimi-amaechis-mother-dies-at-89/

item.title: Kwara: Security Team Kills Two Bandits, Rescues Two Kidnap Victims
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A security team, comprising operatives of DSS and forest guards, has rescued two victims of abduction in a forest between Oba Isin/Owa Onire area of isin and Ifelodun Local Government Areas of Kwara State. The rescued victims are 16-year-old Fatima Azeez and Ibrahim Azeez who is five-year old. The t

item.title: Micron has turned into ‘the most important stock in the market.’ So is it time to worry?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: While investors are concerned about a memory peak, analysts say the stock still looks cheap relative to various earnings scenarios.

item.title: Tinubu unveils $1.25bn World Bank-supported programme
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Thursday launched the over $1.25 billion World Bank-supported human capital development programme aimed at reducing poverty, improving healthcare delivery, and boosting education standards in Nigeria. Speaking while unveiling the programme in Abuja, Tinubu said, &#8220

item.title: Serrano to fight Argentina's Manzur for world titles in TikTok first
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Amanda Serrano will put her world titles on the line against Lucrecia Manzur in California on August 21 in a landmark fight being shown on TikTok.

item.title: Britain's Bearman 'flattered' by Red Bull rumours amid Verstappen uncertainty
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: British driver Oliver Bearman says he is "flattered" by speculation linking him to Red Bull amid ongoing uncertainty over Max Verstappen's future with the team.

item.title: Hamilton: Mercedes should be a lot further ahead in title battle
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lewis Hamilton says Mercedes pair Kimi Antonelli and George Russell "should be a lot further ahead" of him than they are in the Drivers' Championship.

item.title: ‘Respect is important’: If my financial adviser said, ‘Hey,’ my wife and I would walk out. Where do you draw the line?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I met with a financial representative who was not aware that AT&T was the shortened form of American Telephone and Telegraph.”

item.title: Lukman suspends ADC membership, alleges hostile treatment by El-Rufai
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ibrahim Hassan-Wuyo A fresh rift has emerged within the African Democratic Congress (ADC)-led opposition coalition after a prominent member, Salihu Mohammed Lukman, announced the suspension of his membership of the party and the coalition. Lukman, in a message addressed to the National Chairman o

item.title: Author Opens Up On Surviving Alleged Childhood Abuse, Sparks Conversation On African Parenting
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Author and social media commentator behind Dear Naija Girl, Cynthia Osajibenedict, also known as Cynthia Tasha Osajibenedict, has sparked a wave of emotional reactions online after announcing a deeply personal series recounting her experience of alleged childhood abuse while living under the care of

item.title: Infinix Officially Launches the HOT 70 Pro in Nigeria with Active Matrix Cube, IP68 Water Resistance and 5G
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Developed for young creators, students, trendsetters and digital natives, the HOT 70 Pro combines expressive design, immersive personalization, powerful AI capabilities and dependable performance in a smartphone built to help users stand out and stay connected. The launch follows the successful unve

item.title: Two Ekiti Scholars Shine Abroad As Fayemi Harps On Investment In Education
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Former Ekiti State Governor, Dr Kayode Fayemi, has celebrated the achievements of two young Nigerians who benefitted from his administration's education interventions, describing their success as proof of the long-term value of investing in education. The post Two Ekiti Scholars Shine Abroad As Faye

item.title: Most Americans don’t know how to prevent dementia. Here’s what you can do.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: ‘Brain health is a lifelong commitment — it’s never too early or too late to start’

item.title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using "AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] po

item.title: Ecovacs’ self-cleaning Deebot X11 has hit a new low price
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Sometimes it feels like keeping your floors clean is one of those never-ending chores, which is why it's nice to have a versatile robot vacuum take it off your hands. The Ecovacs Deebot X11 robovac / mop hybrid is designed to do just that, and it's now $699 ($400 off) at Amazon and directly from [&#

item.title: Why we rejected an autopsy on Mary Habila despite a controversial death — Family
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: PREMIUM TIMES previously reported that Mary Habila, who was attached to the office of Dave Umahi, was brought dead to the David Umahi Federal University Teaching Hospital, Uburu, Ebonyi State, on 27 June. The post Why we rejected an autopsy on Mary Habila despite a controversial death — Family appea

item.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The same area devastated by floods last year - causing more than 130 deaths, including those at Camp Mystic - is once again being hit.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: England midfielder Jude Bellingham has played down his heated exchange with Lionel Messi during the first half of England's World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, saying the incident was simply a disagreement over a foul. The post What I said to Messi during confrontation – Bellingham appea

item.title: Flood: Akwa Ibom youths demand shift from emergency response to disaster prevention
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: As devastating floods expose glaring preparedness gaps, a youth coalition has asked the Akwa Ibom government to replace its reactive disaster response with a risk-based management framework, following questions over unimplemented flood-control measures despite early warnings and billions of naira in

item.title: Osun Guber: Ooni sues for peace
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Enitan Ogunwusi, has appealed to political parties, candidates, supporters and residents of Osun State to ensure a peaceful and violence-free governorship election in 2026. The monarch made the appeal on Thursday while receiving the All Progressives Congress (APC) govern

item.title: Hollie Doyle blog: Earth Shot can deny Epsom heroine
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Our ambassador gives her verdict on a stellar Saturday card at Newbury, live on Sky Sports Racing, and reveals her thoughts on a Classic weekend at the Curragh.

item.title: Profit-taking in Industrial stocks dips market marginally by 0.02%
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Investors’ profit-taking in industrial stocks dragged the Nigerian equities market lower on Thursday, as the benchmark All-Share Index (ASI) slipped 0.09 per cent to close at 242,145.61 points. The marginal decline trimmed the market’s year-to-date return to 55.61 per cent and reduced investors’ wea

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Lionel Messi paid an emotional tribute to the late Diego Maradona after inspiring Argentina to a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over England, saying the World Cup semi-final triumph was "a gift" for the football legend. item.title: ADC Vice Presidential Candidate Amaechi Loses Mother At 89
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Vice Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Chibuike Rotimi Amaechi, has lost his mother, Mrs. Mary Amaechi.   A statement issued by the Amaechi Media Office on Thursday said Mrs. Amaechi died peacefully on Thursday, July 16, 2026, at the age of 89.

item.title: Mary Habila was not on physiotherapists’ register — NSP
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Society of Physiotherapy (NSP) said on Thursday that the name “Mary Habila” is not listed on its register of licensed physiotherapists, amid continued public scrutiny over the death of the 26-year-old health worker at a residence associated with the Minister of Works, David Umahi. The po

item.title: FG seeks African alliance to bridge telecom gap
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has called on African countries to strengthen collaboration to bridge the continent’s telecommunications gap, warning that nearly one billion Africans are still without access to mobile internet. The post FG seeks African alliance to bridge telecom gap appeared first on The Su

item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has congratulated the Attorney General of the Federation and Minister of Justice, Prince Lateef Olasunkanmi Fagbemi, SAN, as he turned 67th. item.title: Ex-transport minister Rotimi Amaechi’s mother dies at 89

item.title: Kogi High Court Debunks Deregistration Of NDC
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Kogi State High Court, on Thursday, clarified reports claiming that it ordered the deregistration of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC). Clarifying the report, the Court said the widely reported news report linking it to the alleged deregistration did not originate from the Kogi State High Co

item.title: Senate passes bill proposing N50,000 fine for hawking, preaching in commercial vehicles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Senate has approved the Federal Road Safety Corps (Amendment) Bill, 2026, introducing stiffer penalties for several traffic-related offences, including a proposed N50,000 fine for anyone convicted of hawking, trading or preaching inside commercial vehicles. The legislation, which has been forwar

item.title: World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi reveals what Argentina relies on
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, has revealed what his team relies on following their World Cup semi-final 2-1 win against England on Wednesday. Messi helped Argentina by providing two assists as Lionel Scaloni’s side defeated the Three Lions, thanks to goals from Lautaro Martinez and Enzo Fer

item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has restated the commitment of his administration to guarantee the safety of domestic and international investments, declaring that Nigeria, under his leadership, is ready and open for business. The commitment to create an enabling environment for local and foreign capita

item.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20 year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

item.description: The air quality in large parts of Michigan, Minneapolis and Minnesota is deemed "hazardous" by authorities.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Club Brugge’s forward, Christos Tzolis, has reached a full verbal agreement with Arsenal on a long-term contract of at least five years. According to Sky Sports, all personal terms have been agreed. Brugge are now in final negotiations with the Premier League and have already started searching

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Minister of Works, David Umahi, on Thursday said the late Miss Mary Habila, a staff member who died at his residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State, was like a daughter to him. Umahi said this at a press conference in Abuja, insisting that he suspected no foul play and describing attempts to link him to

item.title: NBC tasks broadcasting stations on meeting 2028 DSO global deadline
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigeria Broadcasting Commission (NBC) has stressed the need for broadcasting stations and practitioners nationwide to work towards meeting the 2028 global deadline for Digital Switch Over (DSO). The head of DSO at NBC, Mrs Clementine Wamba, in her presentation titled “DSO Big Picture: Free

item.title: U-20 WWC: Falconets commence Abuja camp ahead Poland 2026
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria’s Falconets have opened camp in Abuja ahead of the 2026 FIFA U-20 Women’s World Cup, DAILY POST reports. Head coach Moses Aduku has invited 28 players to camp as preparations gather momentum for the global show piece. 16 of the invited players are already in camp with the remaining 12

item.title: Two Feared Dead, Several Injured As Gunmen Attack APC Meeting In Osogbo
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: No fewer than two members of the All Progressives Congress (APC) were reportedly killed, while several others sustained gunshot injuries after suspected gunmen attacked a political mobilisation meeting in the Owode-Igbona area of Osogbo, Osun State, on Wednesday night. The attack, which occurred les

item.title: Abiodun hails wife’s compassion, selfless service as she clocks 60
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The governor paid glowing tribute to his wife, describing her as a dependable life partner whose steadfast support, wise counsel, and devotion to family have remained a source of strength over the years The post Abiodun hails wife’s compassion, selfless service as she clocks 60 appeared first on Pre

item.title: BREAKING: Court bars FRSC officials from state roads
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Federal High Court sitting in Kano has ruled that the Federal Road Safety Commission (FRSC) lacks the authority to operate on state and local government roads. The court has also declared the actions in Kano metropolis unlawful and a violation of citizens’ fundamental rights. Delivering judgment

item.title: Appeal Court sets aside judgement nullifying INEC’s 2027 election guidelines
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The appellate court voided and set aside the 20 May judgement of the lower court, which had nullified the guidelines and barred their implementation. The post Appeal Court sets aside judgement nullifying INEC’s 2027 election guidelines appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Apollo Bets on Mexico With $20 Billion Target for Private Credit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. plans to put as much as $20 billion to work financing projects in Mexico as the giant asset manager seeks new outlets for its private credit business.

item.description: ‎‎Nollywood actress Sarah Martins has read the riot act to trolls as she trades words with one of them. ‎‎A female troll took a swipe at the actress for her body, and the actress responded, questioning why trolls don’t look at themselves. Mocking the troll for her obesity, she noted how her legs can

item.title: US govt announces changes to visa for Nigerians from September 2026
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The US Department of Homeland Security, DHS, has introduced a new rule that would impose fixed time limits on visas for Nigerian and other international students, exchange visitors and foreign journalists. This is another step in President Donald Trump’s broader immigration crackdown. In a governmen

item.title: EFCC Re-Arraigns Ex-Skye Bank Chairman Ayeni Over Alleged N15.6bn Fraud
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has re-arraigned former Chairman of the Board of Directors of the defunct Skye Bank Plc, Tunde Ayeni, before the Federal Capital Territory High Court in Apo, Abuja, over an alleged N15.6billion fraud. Ayeni was re-arraigned on Thursday before Justi

item.title: Mary Habila spoke with boyfriend before demise, had nosebleed – Umahi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Minister of Works, David Umahi, has revealed that Mary Habila, the nurse who died at a guest house within his residence in Uburu, Ebonyi State, spoke with her boyfriend shortly before her death and complained of a nosebleed. The post Mary Habila spoke with boyfriend before demise, had nosebleed – Um

item.title: Sons of God and children of Satan, By Chinedu Moghalu
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The familiar claim that we are all God’s children therefore needs to be stated with greater care. We are all God’s creatures, made in His image and dependent upon His goodness. But the New Testament reserves the name children of God for those who receive Christ, are born of God and are led by His [&

item.title: Works Minister Names Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway After Tinubu
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Ministry of Works has named the 750-kilometre Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway after President Bola Ahmed Tinubu in recognition of what it described as his vision and commitment to transformative infrastructure development across the country. Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, announce

item.title: Ogun: Criminals have taken over abandoned Gateway Hotel Ota — Police
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The abandoned Gateway Hotel in Ota, Ogun State, has been described as a safe haven for criminals around the old Sango Toll Gate and its environs. The Area Commander in charge of the Sango Ota Command, ACP Olushola Bamidele, raised the alarm during a one-day working visit to the area. The Commissione

item.title: Security operatives rescue kidnap victims in Kwara South communities
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A security team comprising operatives of the DSS and forest guards on Thursday rescued two victims of abduction in a forest between the Oba Isin/Owa Onire area of Isin/Ifelodun Local Government Areas of Kwara State. Nigerian Tribune gathered that the rescued victims are 16-year-old Fatima Azeez and

item.title: Tinubu Using NBA Election To Test Run 2027 Poll Rigging – Atiku
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in the 2027 presidential election, Alhaji Atiku Abubakar, has accused President Bola Tinubu of meddling in the Nigeria Bar Association (NBA) election. Atiku in a statement by his media office, alleged that the president wants to impose someone o

item.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

item.title: US approves nearly $2bn in weapons sale to Saudi Arabia
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The sale intends to bolster Saudi air defence systems as the kingdom risks escalation with the Iran-backed Houthis.

item.title: Why I Visited Kano NDC Senator – APC Chair
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: He added that Nigeria’s progress would be better achieved through dialogue and cooperation than through political division. The post Why I Visited Kano NDC Senator – APC Chair appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Senate confirms ex-FUOYE VC as ambassador despite sexual harassment allegations
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The committee said the allegations against the nominee had been investigated by both the lawmakers and the university's governing council and were found to be unsubstantiated. The post Senate confirms ex-FUOYE VC as ambassador despite sexual harassment allegations appeared first on Premium Times Nig

item.title: Food inflation remains biggest threat despite easing headline rate – CPPE
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: According to the centre, rising food prices continue to threaten household welfare, calling for structural reforms rather than tighter monetary policy. The post Food inflation remains biggest threat despite easing headline rate – CPPE appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Kogi High Court denies issuing order deregistering NDC
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The court noted that it would ordinarily not have responded to the publication but deemed it necessary to set the records straight. The post Kogi High Court denies issuing order deregistering NDC appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: ITF, NBTE End Long-Running Mandate Dispute, Launch Unified Skills Framework
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Industrial Training Fund (ITF) and the National Board for Technical Education (NBTE) have ended a long-running dispute over their respective mandates, unveiling a unified framework that clearly defines their roles in Nigeria’s skills development architecture and is expected to strengthen t

item.title: AIHS 2026: ESHDC Unveils Demand-Driven Strategy To Tackle Affordable Housing Deficit
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Enugu State Housing Development Corporation (ESHDC) has unveiled a demand-driven approach to affordable housing delivery, advocating practical solutions that align with the needs, preferences, and economic realities of Nigerians. Speaking at the 20th anniversary edition of the Africa Internation

item.title: Hazardous Wildfire Smoke Chokes Millions In US, Canada
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: US states near the Canadian border, including Minnesota, Wisconsin, Michigan, and Illinois, were particularly choked, as forecasters predicted the Northeast, including New York, would also see air quality continue to deteriorate. The post Hazardous Wildfire Smoke Chokes Millions In US, Canada appear

item.title: “The future of African payments will not be one rail. It will be a bridge”– Ivorypay’s Oluwatobi Ajayi says
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Oluwatobi Ajayi built Ivorypay, a stablecoin payment infrastructure provider, on the conviction that Africa’s payment challenge is not…
